title: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media
display_title: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media
wikitext:
''This page is for those references to Evangelion that do not fit into other categories.''  

==Ace Combat 3: Electrosphere==

[[File:Rena Hirose.jpg|thumb|150px|left|thumb|Rena Hirose]]

[[File:Geofront_City_AC3.jpg|150px|right|thumb|Ace Combat 3's Geofront]]

The original Japanese version of this cyberpunk PS1 flight game includes animated cutscenes and designs [https://web.archive.org/web/20231129221823/https://productionig.com/contents/works/05_/000158.html by Production I.G.], responsible for animating some episodes of the Evangelion series as well as much of [[Death and Rebirth]] and [[End of Evangelion]]. Several designs are reminiscent of Eva, but in particular, one of the later missions in the game features an underground city [https://acecombat.fandom.com/wiki/Geofront called the Geofront], which much like [[Geofront| its Eva equivalent]], includes extensive facilities protecting a dark secret, providing the franchise's trademark tunnel flight. Main character [https://acecombat.fandom.com/wiki/Rena_Hirose Rena], a quiet blue-haired pilot with a white flight suit strongly resembles Rei, and she also pilots an aircraft with nerve links, as well as having a somewhat similar backstory. The game's plot partly revolves around an organization named [[Seele|Ouroboros]], which [[Human Instrumentality Project|seeks to upload]] all of humanity's consciousness onto the titular Electrosphere.

==Avatar: The Last Airbender==

According to the audio commentary, the panda spirit Hei Bai was directly inspired by the angels in Evangelion.

==Aoi House==
In volume 1, episode 4, page 19 of the "Original English-language manga", ''Aoi House'', published by Seven Seas Entertainment, there is a mysterious character called ''Oniisan'' which provides Morgan McKnight high-tech equipment that appears as a [[Seele]] Monolith.
[[Image:Aoi House Oniisan.jpg|thumb|none|Oniisan appearing before Morgan as a Seele Monolith]]

==Bayonetta==
The series' antagonistic forces, the Hierarchy of Laguna, have their ranks all comprised of Angels. While their ranks are much more varied than Evangelion's namesake mech cyborg demigods and titular abstract alien abominations, their anatomy, although more heavenly looking, is comprised of unusual figure more akin to angels depicted in medieval sources, such as the Book of Enoch, and have marble exoskeletons that mask their grotesque underlying organic features, which eventually cracks and breaks off during battles. In addition, angels known as Affinities also bear uncanny resemblance to Mass Produced Evangelions found in End of Evangelion.

It should be noted that both Bayonetta and Neon Genesis Evangelion are also endorsed by Sega, and share the same theme song, ''Fly Me To The Moon''.
[[File:MassEvangelionAffinityComparison.jpg|thumb|left|300px|IT'S A CONSPIRACY THE IVATHOLL CORPORATION IS IN CONNECTION WITH NERV AND SEEL-]]
[[File:Angels of Paradiso.jpg|thumb|none|200px|Fly me to the moon, and let me sail amongst the stars~]]

==Danger 5==
Danger 5 is an Australian comedy series about 5 international spies out to kill Hitler. In the 2nd episode of the 2nd season "Johnny Hitler", Ilsa, wears a school uniform that's exactly the same as the ones worn by the female students in Evangelion.

[[File:Danger5 s02e02 uniform.jpg|thumb|none|200px|Ilsa sporting an Evagelion school uniform]]

==Destiny 2==
Destiny 2, a first-person-shooter game developed by Bungie, has a lot of different emotes that you can get from vendors or purchase with real money. There is an emote called "Steepled Stare" where your character sits on a chair, elbows on a table, with your hands up to your mouth like the [[Gendo Ikari]]'s classic pose. It even includes his glasses as part of the emote.

[[File:Destiny2_SteepledStare.jpg|thumb|none|200px|The Gendo Stare]]

==Final Fantasy VII==
Inside a museum is a sign that says "Magma Diver, D-type equipment". One of [[episode 10]]'s titles is "Magma Diver", and the suit [[Evangelion Unit-02]] wears so as to not be harmed by the lava is referred to as D-type equipment.

==Gary and his Demons==
[[Image:GahD-S01E16-ED.jpg|thumb|none|Screenshot of the Season 1, Episode 16 ED]]

In the final episode of season 1 of ''Gary and his Demons''(episode 16), the ending credits, which is normally scrolling credits over a blank black screen, has a short animation that is similar to the Neon Genesis Evangelion ED. [https://wiki.evageeks.org/images/8/8c/Gahd-end-credits-gif.webp Instead of an upside-down Rei spinning it is Gary's sword]. 

==Gravity Falls==
In the series finale, Soos introduces McGucket to anime through a mecha show, where the mecha bares a striking resemblance to an Evangelion.  [https://imgur.com/a/uRMJ6 And when the secret message at the end of the episode's credits is decoded..]

==Justice League (animated series)==
"Legends", a two-parter from the first season, begins with the League fighting a giant robot clearly based the Evangelion designs, mostly [[Eva-02]] (four eyes and smooth head). However, it is a fully mechanical construct controlled by Lex Luthor. (Justice League images courtesy of [http://www.worldsfinestonline.com/ World’s Finest])

Furthermore, [http://www.imdb.com/name/nm0863622/ Bruce Timm], the creator, producer, and character designer for this and other series in the "Detective Comics Animated Universe", has also drawn his own pictures of [https://25.media.tumblr.com/tumblr_lg096axWZ41qzzhvso1_500.jpg Rei] and [https://27.media.tumblr.com/tumblr_lg096axWZ41qzzhvso2_500.jpg Asuka] in his style.

[[Image:Jl_nge_reference_1.jpg|thumb|left|Hawkgirl battles the Eva-styled robot]]
[[Image:Jl_nge_reference_2.jpg|thumb|none|After the battle]]

==Invader Zim==
In episode 9-B, "Hamstergeddon", the camera setup when Ultra Pipi attacks Zim's ship is very similar to the scene in [[Episode 02]] when [[Evangelion Unit-01]] goes berserk. (This similarity was mentioned on the episode's commentary track.) Furthermore, in the episode "Megadoomer", the eponymous mecha's need to constantly be plugged into a power supply was described by series creator Jhonen Vasquez as "Eva-esque", though the idea was not initially an intentional reference.

[[Image:Pipi_charge_1.JPG|thumb|left|Ultra Pipi charges]]
[[Image:Pipi_charge_2.JPG|thumb|none|And again]]


==Koi no Mon==
Suzuki Matsuo's 2004 romantic comedy Koi no Mon features, among its countless anime and manga references, a cosplay bordello that capitalizes on [[Rei Ayanami]]'s fame as an otaku idol. [[Hideaki Anno]] makes a small cameo appearance in the movie as an inn keeper. [http://www.midnighteye.com/reviews/koinomon.shtml Sauce.]
[[Image:Koi no mon cosplay whorehouse.jpg|thumb|left|Some prime Engrish in the brothel's ad.]]
[[Image:Koi no mon anno cameo.jpg|thumb|none|Anno as an inn keeper.]]


==Michael Clayton==
Henry Clayton, the son of the title character, has a large collection of toys and action figures arranged in his room. Two Kaiyodo Evangelion figures, an Eva-02 and an MP Eva, are shown straddling the arm of a desk lamp. 

==Mystique (Marvel)==
Issue #4 of Marvel's ''Mystique'' comic book series briefly features Soviet-built anti-mutant Sentinel robots that look like beefy Evangelions. This is an intentional homage, and the artist Jorge Lucas has admitted being a big Evangelion fan.
[[Image:Mystique jorge lucas.jpg|thumb|none|Article about the 4th Issue of ''Mystique'' and Jorge Lucas]]

==My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic==
In the Season 5 premiere, a pony uses a stick [http://i.imgur.com/0crILmx.png clearly resembling the Spear of Longinus] to strip ponies of their individuality to prevent heartache.  Later that same season, in the episode "Scare Master," Fluttershy creates [http://sailormoonnews.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/12/my_litte_pony_s05e21_sailor_moon_rei_ayanami_utena_bulma_ranma.jpg cardboard cutouts of several anime characters] as ponies, including Rei Ayanami.

==One Hour Photo==

[[Image:One hour photo 3.jpg|thumb|none|Mass Produced Evangelion Figure]]

The character Jake begs his mother to buy him the "Eva" 04 action figure, and Seymour 'Sy' Parrish (played by Robin Williams) later offers it to him for free. It is commonplace for movies and shows to rename or repackage existing products with a generic name and graphic logo. A [[Mass Production Evangelions|Mass Produced Evangelion]] action figure can also be see in the toy store in the movie and the kid incorrectly describes the Mass Produced Eva as being a good guy. Despite the fact that Robin Williams is apparently a "huge fan" of ''Evangelion'' and the toy is an item from Robin Williams' personal collection [http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0265459/trivia], Sy pronounces it "Eh-van-'jell-ee-un". ([http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V5FmFaK7pMU Video Clip]).

==Pantheon==
[[Image:Pantheon-S01E01-Maddies laptop.png|thumb|none|Nerv logo on Maddie's laptop]]

In the animated drama/science fiction series ''Pantheon'', Maddie gets messages claiming to be her dead father. In the beginning of the first episode you can see a ''Nerv''-like logo on her laptop that says "Nerd"

[[Image:Pantheon-S02E02 MaddiesLaptop-zoom.png|thumb|none|Close-up of Maddie's laptop's logo]]

This is a cropped zoom of the logo on Maddie's laptop from season 2, episode 2.

==Partners by Blood==

In episode 7 of the j-drama ''Partners by Blood'', a character answers the question "What made you want to go out with here?" by replying "We got along since we both like Evangelion."<ref>https://gs1.wac.edgecastcdn.net/8019B6/data.tumblr.com/901ea01041af42a7e56b7e53d6d39c56/tumblr_n6bjmayiLE1smuzjvo1_1280.png</ref>

==Penny Arcade==
[[Image:Penny Arcade EVA FF-1000 Poster.jpg|thumb|none|Penny Arcade poster for Sakuracon 2009]]

Webcomic artist Mike Krahulik (Gabe) of [http://www.penny-arcade.com/ Penny Arcade] fame drew an anime themed poster for Sakuracon 2009 featuring stylised characters from the Penny Arcade strip; Gabe is featured as a pilot and the "Fruit Fucker" juicer as his giant robot companion. The Gabe character wears a Eva-esque "plug suit" and the Fruit Fucker features a similar profile to [[Unit-01]], with the horn on Unit-01's helmet becoming the Fruit Fucker's antenna.

==Pooh's Grand Adventure: The Search for Christopher Robin==
[[Image:Poohseele.jpg|thumb|none|Christoper Robin's note; 'Seele' in the upper-right]]

This 1997 Winnie the Pooh movie contains a single shot of Christopher Robin's note covered with honey that has the words "Seele" in it (although that's not part of the note). Probably just a coincidence, although Disney has been known to plagiarize anime (eg. ''Kimba the White Lion'') and Gainax in particular (''Nadia'').

==Regular Show==
In the season six episode "Brilliant Century Duck Crisis Special," the normal opening is replaced with [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n_OC-RAm7Qs a parody of Evangelion's OP].

==Rescue Me==
In the TV Show ''Rescue Me'', shown on the FX channel, Tommy Gavin (played by Denis Leary) is seen in one of the other character's room's which has an "Animerica" poster of the DVD cover of [[The End of Evangelion]].
[[Image:Rescuemeeva.jpg|thumb|none|Animerica poster]]

==Rise of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles==

In the first season finale, a scene references the Hell Train scenes seen throughout the series with Shinji.

[[Image:Tumblr 9e8122ee4fedb3ebd391bc2e563808a8 5115865f 640.jpg|thumb|none|Cowabunga!]]

==Robots==
Fender carries a spear that looks similar to the [[Spear of Longinus]]. Of course, Fender is voiced by Robin Williams.

==Steven Universe==
The ending of "The Test" pastiches the the "Congratulations!" scene from [[Episode 26]] by having Steven be congratulated by the other characters as a minimalist version of the theme song plays.  Later, in the episode "Joy Ride," [http://webmup.com/m12wm/ this happened.]

==Scott Pilgrim==
Envy Adams' hair [http://radiomaru.tumblr.com/post/52484006420/kinda-just-a-weird-coincidence-that-envy-adams was inspired] by that of [[Asuka]].  Also, in the video game, Todd Ingram's force field resembles an A.T. field.

==South Park==
A character resembling Misato appears alongisde several Japanese in an "anime" scene from Season 17, Episode 9. This was likely an in-joke on the part of an animator, and not a member of the show's core creative team.

[[File:MisatoSouthPark.jpeg|thumb|200px|none|Purinsesu Kenny...]]

==Super Robot Taisen: Original Generation==
Even though the Original Generation incarnations of the ''Super Robot Wars'' game franchise only feature characters created by its developer company Banpresto instead of the usual crossover ensemble casts, these games sometimes make humorous references to mecha anime (most notably Gundam).

In scenario 8 of ''Original Generation'' there is an apparent reference to Evangelion's [[Episode 19]], as Ryusei [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oN0IlOcBykQ tries to convince his mech to cooperate by screaming at it]. This scene is later repeated in scenario 16 of ''Original Generation 2'', when Ryoto attempts to activate the Huckebein Mk-III under enemy fire.

==Teen Titans==
In Teen Titans: Trouble in Tokyo, the design and the sound effects of the elevator to the Trooper HQ are identical to the elevator going into the Geofront. A logo that looks similar to Nerv can be seen on a truck pasting by in the movie.

==The Day the Earth Stood Still==
In the 2008 remake of ''The Day the Earth Stood Still'' starring Keanu Reeves, In the beginning of the movie where you see a Evangelion Unit 01 action figure is seen in Jacob's (Played by Jaden Smith) room when Helen Bens (Played by Jennifer Connelly) walks in just before the unknown alien spaceship crash course to the Earth. (Incidentally, the title of [[episode 11]] is a shout out to the original movie.)

==Train Man (Densha Otoko)==
The TV drama adaptation of ''Train Man'' contains numerous Evangelion references, including a stoner fanboy wearing Rei's plugsuit. (Not surprisingly, Mobile Suit Gundam is another show [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mU2SGVA3qjQ that gets a specific, super-creepy fanboy.])

Incidentally, the opening of the drama [http://bluemist.animeblogger.net/archives/densha-otoko-and-daicon-iv/ is based on the Daicon IV opening animation] by the original Gainax core staff.

[[Image:Densha eva fanboy.jpg|thumb|left|Typical 2channeler Evangelion fan]]
[[Image:Densha unit00 still looks the best.jpg|thumb|none|]]


==Welder==
The musician known as Welder has employed sound clips from the End of Evangelion in one of his songs, "Find You Here." The sound clips in question come from the scene when Gendo first meets Rei in order to begin his version of Third Impact: "I knew I'd find you here," and "The time has come. Let's go." 
{{-}}
==References==
{{reflist}}
{{Tributes Box}}

[[Category:Comparison Pages]]
[[Category:Guides]]

Ace Combat 3: Electrosphere[edit]

The original Japanese version of this cyberpunk PS1 flight game includes animated cutscenes and designs by Production I.G., responsible for animating some episodes of the Evangelion series as well as much of Death and Rebirth and End of Evangelion. Several designs are reminiscent of Eva, but in particular, one of the later missions in the game features an underground city called the Geofront, which much like its Eva equivalent, includes extensive facilities protecting a dark secret, providing the franchise's trademark tunnel flight. Main character Rena, a quiet blue-haired pilot with a white flight suit strongly resembles Rei, and she also pilots an aircraft with nerve links, as well as having a somewhat similar backstory. The game's plot partly revolves around an organization named Ouroboros, which seeks to upload all of humanity's consciousness onto the titular Electrosphere.

Avatar: The Last Airbender[edit]

According to the audio commentary, the panda spirit Hei Bai was directly inspired by the angels in Evangelion.

Aoi House[edit]

In volume 1, episode 4, page 19 of the "Original English-language manga", Aoi House, published by Seven Seas Entertainment, there is a mysterious character called Oniisan which provides Morgan McKnight high-tech equipment that appears as a Seele Monolith.

Bayonetta[edit]

The series' antagonistic forces, the Hierarchy of Laguna, have their ranks all comprised of Angels. While their ranks are much more varied than Evangelion's namesake mech cyborg demigods and titular abstract alien abominations, their anatomy, although more heavenly looking, is comprised of unusual figure more akin to angels depicted in medieval sources, such as the Book of Enoch, and have marble exoskeletons that mask their grotesque underlying organic features, which eventually cracks and breaks off during battles. In addition, angels known as Affinities also bear uncanny resemblance to Mass Produced Evangelions found in End of Evangelion.

It should be noted that both Bayonetta and Neon Genesis Evangelion are also endorsed by Sega, and share the same theme song, Fly Me To The Moon.

Danger 5[edit]

Danger 5 is an Australian comedy series about 5 international spies out to kill Hitler. In the 2nd episode of the 2nd season "Johnny Hitler", Ilsa, wears a school uniform that's exactly the same as the ones worn by the female students in Evangelion.

Destiny 2[edit]

Destiny 2, a first-person-shooter game developed by Bungie, has a lot of different emotes that you can get from vendors or purchase with real money. There is an emote called "Steepled Stare" where your character sits on a chair, elbows on a table, with your hands up to your mouth like the Gendo Ikari's classic pose. It even includes his glasses as part of the emote.

Final Fantasy VII[edit]

Inside a museum is a sign that says "Magma Diver, D-type equipment". One of episode 10's titles is "Magma Diver", and the suit Evangelion Unit-02 wears so as to not be harmed by the lava is referred to as D-type equipment.

Gary and his Demons[edit]

In the final episode of season 1 of Gary and his Demons(episode 16), the ending credits, which is normally scrolling credits over a blank black screen, has a short animation that is similar to the Neon Genesis Evangelion ED. Instead of an upside-down Rei spinning it is Gary's sword.

Gravity Falls[edit]

In the series finale, Soos introduces McGucket to anime through a mecha show, where the mecha bares a striking resemblance to an Evangelion. And when the secret message at the end of the episode's credits is decoded..

Justice League (animated series)[edit]

"Legends", a two-parter from the first season, begins with the League fighting a giant robot clearly based the Evangelion designs, mostly Eva-02 (four eyes and smooth head). However, it is a fully mechanical construct controlled by Lex Luthor. (Justice League images courtesy of World’s Finest)

Furthermore, Bruce Timm, the creator, producer, and character designer for this and other series in the "Detective Comics Animated Universe", has also drawn his own pictures of Rei and Asuka in his style.

Invader Zim[edit]

In episode 9-B, "Hamstergeddon", the camera setup when Ultra Pipi attacks Zim's ship is very similar to the scene in Episode 02 when Evangelion Unit-01 goes berserk. (This similarity was mentioned on the episode's commentary track.) Furthermore, in the episode "Megadoomer", the eponymous mecha's need to constantly be plugged into a power supply was described by series creator Jhonen Vasquez as "Eva-esque", though the idea was not initially an intentional reference.


Koi no Mon[edit]

Suzuki Matsuo's 2004 romantic comedy Koi no Mon features, among its countless anime and manga references, a cosplay bordello that capitalizes on Rei Ayanami's fame as an otaku idol. Hideaki Anno makes a small cameo appearance in the movie as an inn keeper. Sauce.


Michael Clayton[edit]

Henry Clayton, the son of the title character, has a large collection of toys and action figures arranged in his room. Two Kaiyodo Evangelion figures, an Eva-02 and an MP Eva, are shown straddling the arm of a desk lamp.

Mystique (Marvel)[edit]

Issue #4 of Marvel's Mystique comic book series briefly features Soviet-built anti-mutant Sentinel robots that look like beefy Evangelions. This is an intentional homage, and the artist Jorge Lucas has admitted being a big Evangelion fan.

My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic[edit]

In the Season 5 premiere, a pony uses a stick clearly resembling the Spear of Longinus to strip ponies of their individuality to prevent heartache. Later that same season, in the episode "Scare Master," Fluttershy creates cardboard cutouts of several anime characters as ponies, including Rei Ayanami.

One Hour Photo[edit]

The character Jake begs his mother to buy him the "Eva" 04 action figure, and Seymour 'Sy' Parrish (played by Robin Williams) later offers it to him for free. It is commonplace for movies and shows to rename or repackage existing products with a generic name and graphic logo. A Mass Produced Evangelion action figure can also be see in the toy store in the movie and the kid incorrectly describes the Mass Produced Eva as being a good guy. Despite the fact that Robin Williams is apparently a "huge fan" of Evangelion and the toy is an item from Robin Williams' personal collection [1], Sy pronounces it "Eh-van-'jell-ee-un". (Video Clip).

Pantheon[edit]

In the animated drama/science fiction series Pantheon, Maddie gets messages claiming to be her dead father. In the beginning of the first episode you can see a Nerv-like logo on her laptop that says "Nerd"

This is a cropped zoom of the logo on Maddie's laptop from season 2, episode 2.

Partners by Blood[edit]

In episode 7 of the j-drama Partners by Blood, a character answers the question "What made you want to go out with here?" by replying "We got along since we both like Evangelion."[1]

Penny Arcade[edit]

Webcomic artist Mike Krahulik (Gabe) of Penny Arcade fame drew an anime themed poster for Sakuracon 2009 featuring stylised characters from the Penny Arcade strip; Gabe is featured as a pilot and the "Fruit Fucker" juicer as his giant robot companion. The Gabe character wears a Eva-esque "plug suit" and the Fruit Fucker features a similar profile to Unit-01, with the horn on Unit-01's helmet becoming the Fruit Fucker's antenna.

Pooh's Grand Adventure: The Search for Christopher Robin[edit]

This 1997 Winnie the Pooh movie contains a single shot of Christopher Robin's note covered with honey that has the words "Seele" in it (although that's not part of the note). Probably just a coincidence, although Disney has been known to plagiarize anime (eg. Kimba the White Lion) and Gainax in particular (Nadia).

Regular Show[edit]

In the season six episode "Brilliant Century Duck Crisis Special," the normal opening is replaced with a parody of Evangelion's OP.

Rescue Me[edit]

In the TV Show Rescue Me, shown on the FX channel, Tommy Gavin (played by Denis Leary) is seen in one of the other character's room's which has an "Animerica" poster of the DVD cover of The End of Evangelion.

Rise of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles[edit]

In the first season finale, a scene references the Hell Train scenes seen throughout the series with Shinji.

Robots[edit]

Fender carries a spear that looks similar to the Spear of Longinus. Of course, Fender is voiced by Robin Williams.

Steven Universe[edit]

The ending of "The Test" pastiches the the "Congratulations!" scene from Episode 26 by having Steven be congratulated by the other characters as a minimalist version of the theme song plays. Later, in the episode "Joy Ride," this happened.

Scott Pilgrim[edit]

Envy Adams' hair was inspired by that of Asuka. Also, in the video game, Todd Ingram's force field resembles an A.T. field.

South Park[edit]

A character resembling Misato appears alongisde several Japanese in an "anime" scene from Season 17, Episode 9. This was likely an in-joke on the part of an animator, and not a member of the show's core creative team.

Super Robot Taisen: Original Generation[edit]

Even though the Original Generation incarnations of the Super Robot Wars game franchise only feature characters created by its developer company Banpresto instead of the usual crossover ensemble casts, these games sometimes make humorous references to mecha anime (most notably Gundam).

In scenario 8 of Original Generation there is an apparent reference to Evangelion's Episode 19, as Ryusei tries to convince his mech to cooperate by screaming at it. This scene is later repeated in scenario 16 of Original Generation 2, when Ryoto attempts to activate the Huckebein Mk-III under enemy fire.

Teen Titans[edit]

In Teen Titans: Trouble in Tokyo, the design and the sound effects of the elevator to the Trooper HQ are identical to the elevator going into the Geofront. A logo that looks similar to Nerv can be seen on a truck pasting by in the movie.

The Day the Earth Stood Still[edit]

In the 2008 remake of The Day the Earth Stood Still starring Keanu Reeves, In the beginning of the movie where you see a Evangelion Unit 01 action figure is seen in Jacob's (Played by Jaden Smith) room when Helen Bens (Played by Jennifer Connelly) walks in just before the unknown alien spaceship crash course to the Earth. (Incidentally, the title of episode 11 is a shout out to the original movie.)

Train Man (Densha Otoko)[edit]

The TV drama adaptation of Train Man contains numerous Evangelion references, including a stoner fanboy wearing Rei's plugsuit. (Not surprisingly, Mobile Suit Gundam is another show that gets a specific, super-creepy fanboy.)

Incidentally, the opening of the drama is based on the Daicon IV opening animation by the original Gainax core staff.


Welder[edit]

The musician known as Welder has employed sound clips from the End of Evangelion in one of his songs, "Find You Here." The sound clips in question come from the scene when Gendo first meets Rei in order to begin his version of Third Impact: "I knew I'd find you here," and "The time has come. Let's go."

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media
This page is for those references to Evangelion that do not fit into other categories.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Ace Combat 3: Electrosphere
thumb|150px|left|thumb|Rena Hirose

150px|right|thumb|Ace Combat 3's Geofront

The original Japanese version of this cyberpunk PS1 flight game includes animated cutscenes and designs by Production I.G., responsible for animating some episodes of the Evangelion series as well as much of Death and Rebirth and End of Evangelion. Several designs are reminiscent of Eva, but in particular, one of the later missions in the game features an underground city called the Geofront, which much like  its Eva equivalent, includes extensive facilities protecting a dark secret, providing the franchise's trademark tunnel flight. Main character Rena, a quiet blue-haired pilot with a white flight suit strongly resembles Rei, and she also pilots an aircraft with nerve links, as well as having a somewhat similar backstory. The game's plot partly revolves around an organization named Ouroboros, which seeks to upload all of humanity's consciousness onto the titular Electrosphere.

According to the audio commentary, the panda spirit Hei Bai was directly inspired by the angels in Evangelion.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Bayonetta
The series' antagonistic forces, the Hierarchy of Laguna, have their ranks all comprised of Angels. While their ranks are much more varied than Evangelion's namesake mech cyborg demigods and titular abstract alien abominations, their anatomy, although more heavenly looking, is comprised of unusual figure more akin to angels depicted in medieval sources, such as the Book of Enoch, and have marble exoskeletons that mask their grotesque underlying organic features, which eventually cracks and breaks off during battles. In addition, angels known as Affinities also bear uncanny resemblance to Mass Produced Evangelions found in End of Evangelion.

It should be noted that both Bayonetta and Neon Genesis Evangelion are also endorsed by Sega, and share the same theme song, Fly Me To The Moon.
thumb|left|300px|IT'S A CONSPIRACY THE IVATHOLL CORPORATION IS IN CONNECTION WITH NERV AND SEEL-
thumb|none|200px|Fly me to the moon, and let me sail amongst the stars~

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Danger 5
Danger 5 is an Australian comedy series about 5 international spies out to kill Hitler. In the 2nd episode of the 2nd season "Johnny Hitler", Ilsa, wears a school uniform that's exactly the same as the ones worn by the female students in Evangelion.

thumb|none|200px|Ilsa sporting an Evagelion school uniform

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Destiny 2
Destiny 2, a first-person-shooter game developed by Bungie, has a lot of different emotes that you can get from vendors or purchase with real money. There is an emote called "Steepled Stare" where your character sits on a chair, elbows on a table, with your hands up to your mouth like the Gendo Ikari's classic pose. It even includes his glasses as part of the emote.

thumb|none|200px|The Gendo Stare

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Final Fantasy VII
Inside a museum is a sign that says "Magma Diver, D-type equipment". One of episode 10's titles is "Magma Diver", and the suit Evangelion Unit-02 wears so as to not be harmed by the lava is referred to as D-type equipment.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Gary and his Demons
thumb|none|Screenshot of the Season 1, Episode 16 ED

In the final episode of season 1 of Gary and his Demons(episode 16), the ending credits, which is normally scrolling credits over a blank black screen, has a short animation that is similar to the Neon Genesis Evangelion ED. Instead of an upside-down Rei spinning it is Gary's sword.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Gravity Falls
In the series finale, Soos introduces McGucket to anime through a mecha show, where the mecha bares a striking resemblance to an Evangelion.  And when the secret message at the end of the episode's credits is decoded..

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Justice League (animated series)
"Legends", a two-parter from the first season, begins with the League fighting a giant robot clearly based the Evangelion designs, mostly Eva-02 (four eyes and smooth head). However, it is a fully mechanical construct controlled by Lex Luthor. (Justice League images courtesy of World’s Finest)

Furthermore, Bruce Timm, the creator, producer, and character designer for this and other series in the "Detective Comics Animated Universe", has also drawn his own pictures of Rei and Asuka in his style.

thumb|left|Hawkgirl battles the Eva-styled robot
thumb|none|After the battle

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Invader Zim
Invader Zim
In episode 9-B, "Hamstergeddon", the camera setup when Ultra Pipi attacks Zim's ship is very similar to the scene in Episode 02 when Evangelion Unit-01 goes berserk. (This similarity was mentioned on the episode's commentary track.) Furthermore, in the episode "Megadoomer", the eponymous mecha's need to constantly be plugged into a power supply was described by series creator Jhonen Vasquez as "Eva-esque", though the idea was not initially an intentional reference.

thumb|left|Ultra Pipi charges
thumb|none|And again

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Koi no Mon
Koi no Mon
Suzuki Matsuo's 2004 romantic comedy Koi no Mon features, among its countless anime and manga references, a cosplay bordello that capitalizes on Rei Ayanami's fame as an otaku idol. Hideaki Anno makes a small cameo appearance in the movie as an inn keeper. Sauce.
thumb|left|Some prime Engrish in the brothel's ad.
thumb|none|Anno as an inn keeper.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Michael Clayton
Michael Clayton
Henry Clayton, the son of the title character, has a large collection of toys and action figures arranged in his room. Two Kaiyodo Evangelion figures, an Eva-02 and an MP Eva, are shown straddling the arm of a desk lamp.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Mystique (Marvel)
Mystique (Marvel)
Issue #4 of Marvel's Mystique comic book series briefly features Soviet-built anti-mutant Sentinel robots that look like beefy Evangelions. This is an intentional homage, and the artist Jorge Lucas has admitted being a big Evangelion fan.
thumb|none|Article about the 4th Issue of Mystique and Jorge Lucas

chunks.section: My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic
chunks.content:
Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic
My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic
In the Season 5 premiere, a pony uses a stick clearly resembling the Spear of Longinus to strip ponies of their individuality to prevent heartache.  Later that same season, in the episode "Scare Master," Fluttershy creates cardboard cutouts of several anime characters as ponies, including Rei Ayanami.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: One Hour Photo
One Hour Photo

thumb|none|Mass Produced Evangelion Figure

The character Jake begs his mother to buy him the "Eva" 04 action figure, and Seymour 'Sy' Parrish (played by Robin Williams) later offers it to him for free. It is commonplace for movies and shows to rename or repackage existing products with a generic name and graphic logo. A Mass Produced Evangelion action figure can also be see in the toy store in the movie and the kid incorrectly describes the Mass Produced Eva as being a good guy. Despite the fact that Robin Williams is apparently a "huge fan" of Evangelion and the toy is an item from Robin Williams' personal collection , Sy pronounces it "Eh-van-'jell-ee-un". (Video Clip).

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Pantheon
Pantheon
thumb|none|Nerv logo on Maddie's laptop

In the animated drama/science fiction series Pantheon, Maddie gets messages claiming to be her dead father. In the beginning of the first episode you can see a Nerv-like logo on her laptop that says "Nerd"

thumb|none|Close-up of Maddie's laptop's logo

This is a cropped zoom of the logo on Maddie's laptop from season 2, episode 2.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Partners by Blood
Partners by Blood

In episode 7 of the j-drama Partners by Blood, a character answers the question "What made you want to go out with here?" by replying "We got along since we both like Evangelion."https://gs1.wac.edgecastcdn.net/8019B6/data.tumblr.com/901ea01041af42a7e56b7e53d6d39c56/tumblr_n6bjmayiLE1smuzjvo1_1280.png

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Penny Arcade
Penny Arcade
thumb|none|Penny Arcade poster for Sakuracon 2009

Webcomic artist Mike Krahulik (Gabe) of Penny Arcade fame drew an anime themed poster for Sakuracon 2009 featuring stylised characters from the Penny Arcade strip; Gabe is featured as a pilot and the "Fruit Fucker" juicer as his giant robot companion. The Gabe character wears a Eva-esque "plug suit" and the Fruit Fucker features a similar profile to Unit-01, with the horn on Unit-01's helmet becoming the Fruit Fucker's antenna.

chunks.section: Pooh's Grand Adventure: The Search for Christopher Robin
chunks.content:
Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Pooh's Grand Adventure: The Search for Christopher Robin
Pooh's Grand Adventure: The Search for Christopher Robin
thumb|none|Christoper Robin's note; 'Seele' in the upper-right

This 1997 Winnie the Pooh movie contains a single shot of Christopher Robin's note covered with honey that has the words "Seele" in it (although that's not part of the note). Probably just a coincidence, although Disney has been known to plagiarize anime (eg. Kimba the White Lion) and Gainax in particular (Nadia).

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Regular Show
Regular Show
In the season six episode "Brilliant Century Duck Crisis Special," the normal opening is replaced with a parody of Evangelion's OP.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Rescue Me
Rescue Me
In the TV Show Rescue Me, shown on the FX channel, Tommy Gavin (played by Denis Leary) is seen in one of the other character's room's which has an "Animerica" poster of the DVD cover of The End of Evangelion.
thumb|none|Animerica poster

chunks.section: Rise of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
chunks.content:
Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Rise of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
Rise of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

In the first season finale, a scene references the Hell Train scenes seen throughout the series with Shinji.

thumb|none|Cowabunga!

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Robots
Robots
Fender carries a spear that looks similar to the Spear of Longinus. Of course, Fender is voiced by Robin Williams.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Steven Universe
Steven Universe
The ending of "The Test" pastiches the the "Congratulations!" scene from Episode 26 by having Steven be congratulated by the other characters as a minimalist version of the theme song plays.  Later, in the episode "Joy Ride," this happened.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Scott Pilgrim
Scott Pilgrim
Envy Adams' hair was inspired by that of Asuka.  Also, in the video game, Todd Ingram's force field resembles an A.T. field.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: South Park
South Park
A character resembling Misato appears alongisde several Japanese in an "anime" scene from Season 17, Episode 9. This was likely an in-joke on the part of an animator, and not a member of the show's core creative team.

thumb|200px|none|Purinsesu Kenny...

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Super Robot Taisen: Original Generation
Super Robot Taisen: Original Generation
Even though the Original Generation incarnations of the Super Robot Wars game franchise only feature characters created by its developer company Banpresto instead of the usual crossover ensemble casts, these games sometimes make humorous references to mecha anime (most notably Gundam).

In scenario 8 of Original Generation there is an apparent reference to Evangelion's Episode 19, as Ryusei tries to convince his mech to cooperate by screaming at it. This scene is later repeated in scenario 16 of Original Generation 2, when Ryoto attempts to activate the Huckebein Mk-III under enemy fire.

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Teen Titans
Teen Titans
In Teen Titans: Trouble in Tokyo, the design and the sound effects of the elevator to the Trooper HQ are identical to the elevator going into the Geofront. A logo that looks similar to Nerv can be seen on a truck pasting by in the movie.

chunks.section: The Day the Earth Stood Still
chunks.content:
Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: The Day the Earth Stood Still
The Day the Earth Stood Still
In the 2008 remake of The Day the Earth Stood Still starring Keanu Reeves, In the beginning of the movie where you see a Evangelion Unit 01 action figure is seen in Jacob's (Played by Jaden Smith) room when Helen Bens (Played by Jennifer Connelly) walks in just before the unknown alien spaceship crash course to the Earth. (Incidentally, the title of episode 11 is a shout out to the original movie.)

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Train Man (Densha Otoko)
Train Man (Densha Otoko)
The TV drama adaptation of Train Man contains numerous Evangelion references, including a stoner fanboy wearing Rei's plugsuit. (Not surprisingly, Mobile Suit Gundam is another show that gets a specific, super-creepy fanboy.)

Incidentally, the opening of the drama is based on the Daicon IV opening animation by the original Gainax core staff.

thumb|left|Typical 2channeler Evangelion fan
thumb|none|

Article: Tributes to Neon Genesis Evangelion in other Media | Section: Welder
Welder
The musician known as Welder has employed sound clips from the End of Evangelion in one of his songs, "Find You Here." The sound clips in question come from the scene when Gendo first meets Rei in order to begin his version of Third Impact: "I knew I'd find you here," and "The time has come. Let's go." 
{{-}
